name: Lake Eychelle and Eychelle Hut Trail
name_original: Etang d’Eychelle – randonnée au départ du Col de la Core – Réserve domaniale du mont Valier – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
description: Hike to the picturesque Lake Eychelle from Col de la Core, covering 5.3 miles (8.5 km) with 2264 feet (690 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Sentenac-D'Oust offers access to diverse Pyrenean landscapes, characterized by high peaks, forests, pastures, and gushing streams. The region is part of the Natural Regional Park of the Ariège Pyrenees and features varied terrain suitable for different hiking abilities. It also includes sections of the GR10 long-distance hiking trail, providing extensive exploration opportunities. Best hiking trails around Sentenac-D'Oust The most popular hiking route is Lake Eychelle and Eychelle Hut Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the mountain lake of Eychelle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Core Pass – Lake Bethmale loop from Col de la Core, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This loop offers views of the mountains and valleys around the emblematic Col de la Core. Local hikers also love the Col de la Core – Lake Eychelle loop from Col de la Core, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through Pyrenean scenery, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Sentenac-D'Oust is defined by Pyrenean peaks, forests, mountain passes like Col de la Core, and diverse flora and fauna. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging alpine treks. The routes in Sentenac-D'Oust are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 290 reviews. More than 2900 hikers have used komoot to explore Sentenac-D'Oust's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;15 distinct hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Sentenac-D'Oust, offering a variety of experiences. These include 1 easy, 8 moderate, and 6 difficult routes, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: Hiking in Sentenac-D'Oust offers diverse Pyrenean landscapes, including high peaks, dense forests, and open pastures used for summer grazing. You'll encounter gushing streams and varied flora and fauna, especially around mountain passes like Col d'Auedole. The region is part of the Natural Regional Park of the Ariège Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: Yes, there is &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt; available in Sentenac-D'Oust, perfect for beginners or families looking for a gentle introduction to the Pyrenean scenery. While specific family-friendly features like playgrounds aren't detailed, the easy routes generally offer accessible paths.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can visit the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/673275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayes Pond&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3106151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascade de Nerech&lt;/a&gt; (waterfall). Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass&lt;/a&gt; and Col d'Auedole offer superb views and rich biodiversity, including unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3100802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;flora and fauna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10530632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass – Lake Bethmale loop from Col de la Core&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.8 km long. For a more challenging experience, consider the 11.1 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15014754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Core – Lake Eychelle loop from Col de la Core&lt;/a&gt;, or the extensive 21.9 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37595981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gîte of Esbintz loop from Cabanes de Casabede - castillonais&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: The hiking routes in Sentenac-D'Oust are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from around 290 reviews. Hikers often praise the breathtaking Pyrenean scenery, the diverse terrain, and the well-maintained paths that lead to stunning natural features like Lake Eychelle.

Q: Can I access the GR®10 long-distance hiking trail from Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: Yes, Sentenac-D'Oust is a key point on the legendary &lt;strong&gt;GR®10 long-distance hiking trail&lt;/strong&gt;, which traverses the Pyrenees. You can access sections of this trail, offering immense beauty and diverse landscapes, including routes leading to Lake Eychelle from Col de la Core or Col d'Auedole.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Sentenac-D'Oust offers &lt;strong&gt;6 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced trekkers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15014754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Core – Lake Eychelle loop from Col de la Core&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.1 km with 661 meters of elevation gain. For a truly demanding trek, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37595981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gîte of Esbintz loop from Cabanes de Casabede - castillonais&lt;/a&gt; covers 21.9 km with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: The region is generally best for hiking from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is more favorable, and the trails are typically clear of snow, allowing you to fully enjoy the rich flora and fauna. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate alpine experience and gear due to snow and challenging conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any mountain passes I can hike over in the area?
A: Yes, Sentenac-D'Oust is surrounded by several notable mountain passes. The emblematic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass&lt;/a&gt; (Col de la Core) is a popular spot for hikers, offering superb views. Other passes include Col d'Auedole, known for its rich biodiversity, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2685946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Saraillé&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1545103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Portech&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1545119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Catchaudégué Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3104691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Pouech&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there accommodation available for hikers along the trails?
A: Yes, for those undertaking longer treks, the &lt;strong&gt;Gîte of Esbintz&lt;/strong&gt; is a magnificent lodge that serves as a crossroads for transhumance and itinerant routes, offering accommodation for hikers. It's a crucial point for multi-day excursions in the area.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on hikes in Sentenac-D'Oust?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly across the trails. Easy paths might have minimal gain, while moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958318910/lake-eychelle-and-eychelle-hut-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Eychelle and Eychelle Hut Trail&lt;/a&gt; involve around 690 meters of ascent. Difficult hikes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37595981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gîte of Esbintz loop&lt;/a&gt;, can feature over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, requiring good fitness and sturdy shoes.
tips: The Col de la Core, at 1,395 meters above sea level, is part of both the pass route and the GR10 which cross the Pyrenees from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean.
You meet the lake shortly before you get back on the GR10.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Etang d’Eychelle hike?
faqs.answer: The hike starts from Col de la Core, which is a mountain pass. Parking is generally available directly at the pass, serving as the trailhead for this route. It's part of the 'route des cols' across the Pyrenees, making it an accessible starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Etang d’Eychelle hike dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail as it's within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises. However, please be aware that you might encounter Pyrenees Mountain dogs (Patous) guarding flocks in summer pastures. It's crucial to keep your dog on a leash and avoid disturbing livestock or wildlife. Always respect local regulations for protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to Etang d’Eychelle?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike to Lake Eychelle is typically from late spring to early autumn (roughly June to September). During this period, the weather is more stable, and you can enjoy wildflowers and see sheep and cows grazing near Cabane d'Eychelle. Winter conditions can make the trail challenging due to snow and ice, requiring specialized equipment and experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike to Etang d’Eychelle. The trail is located within the Réserve domaniale du Mont Valier and the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, which are protected areas. While there are no fees, hikers are expected to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and follow general park regulations to preserve its wild beauty.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail to Etang d’Eychelle?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and generally moderate. It starts by climbing through a meadow, then passes through beech forests and an avalanche corridor. The path includes grassy areas, forest paths, and sections with earth, pebbles, and rock. The main climb features a significant elevation gain, especially over the last 3 kilometers, with an average gradient of about 17%. Some parts can be steep, and walking poles are recommended for the descent.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Etang d’Eychelle hike?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the starting point at Col de la Core with views of the Bethmale Valley, picturesque beech forests, and the stunning destination of Lake Eychelle. Near the lake, you'll find Cabane d'Eychelle, a shepherd's hut, offering a glimpse into local pastoral life and a scenic resting spot.

faqs.question: Is the hike to Etang d’Eychelle suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This hike is rated as moderate. While relatively short (around 8.5 km), it involves a significant elevation gain of about 690 meters, particularly a steep section towards the lake. Good fitness and sure-footedness are required. It might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners, but active families with hiking experience could manage it. Consider the fitness level of all participants before attempting.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Etang d’Eychelle hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this hike is approximately 4 hours and 15 minutes. This accounts for the 8.5 km distance and 690 meters of elevation gain. Actual time may vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and time spent enjoying the scenery at Lake Eychelle and Cabane d'Eychelle.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the initial part of this hike follows the well-known GR®10D trail. Additionally, a small section (8%) of the route overlaps with 'La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.4', offering connections to longer-distance paths for those looking to extend their adventure.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, you can expect rich biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various wildflowers during the right season. Wildlife sightings are possible, and you might encounter sheep and cows grazing in the summer pastures, often accompanied by Pyrenees Mountain dogs. Always observe from a distance and avoid disturbing animals.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or recommendations for this hike?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for a significant and sustained climb, especially from the second kilometer onwards, with an average gradient of about 17% over the last 3 kilometers to the lake. We highly recommend bringing walking poles, particularly for the descent, to aid stability and reduce strain on your knees. Good hiking boots with ankle support are also advisable due to the varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to get refreshments near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trailhead is located at Col de la Core, a mountain pass. While there might not be extensive facilities directly at the pass, you can typically find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in nearby villages within the Bethmale Valley or the wider Couserans region. It's best to check local tourism websites like tourisme-couserans-pyrenees.com for current information on services before your hike.
